items.title: Rangers gooit Schotse titelstrijd helemaal open
items.summary: Rangers FC heeft Heart of Midlothian een behoorlijke dreun uitgedeeld. In de Schotse kraker kwam Hearts tot twee keer toe op voorsprong, maar uiteindelijk was het de thuisploeg die op Ibrox met de drie punten aan de haal ging. Daarmee is de Schotse titelstrijd weer helemaal open, waar ook Celtic zich inmengt.

items.title: Arsenal 4-0 Wigan Athletic: FA Cup fourth round – live
items.summary: ⚽ FA Cup news from the 4.30pm GMT kick-off in London ⚽ Live scoreboard | Follow us on Bluesky | And mail John Breaking: Riccardo Calafiori got hurt in the warmup so Bukayo Saka is a starter, and captain. MLS in midfield looks like it will be an experiment for another day. Sunday’s FA Cup matches already. Continue reading...

items.title: PEC Zwolle van 'Barcelona naar amateurs': 'Dat is mindset'
items.summary: PEC Zwolle speelde zondag een fantastisch half uur op bezoek bij SC Heerenveen, maar gaf in het laatste uur niet thuis en verloor uiteindelijk ruim in het Abe Lenstra Stadion . Hoe is het enorme verval te verklaren?

items.title: 'A throwback' - Wolves beat Grimsby in FA Cup mudbath
items.summary: Premier League Wolves edge League Two Grimsby in an "old school cup tie" which provided an FA Cup "throwback".

items.title: James instrumental in Chelsea defeat over Liverpool
items.summary: Lauren James is instrumental in Chelsea's 2-0 win over Liverpool as she assists Nusken before scoring her side's second.

items.title: VI Live: Malen gaat op bezoek met AS Roma bij Napoli
items.summary: Zondag staan er vier wedstrijden in de Eredivisie op het programma. Het duel van Feyenoord volgen we in een aparte live. In deze VI Live blijf je op de hoogte van SC Heerenveen tegen PEC Zwolle, Telstar tegen FC Twente, Sparta Rotterdam tegen NEC én het belangrijkste buitenlandse voetbal.

items.title: Wervelend Arsenal stelt binnen halfuur plek in achtste finale FA Cup veilig
items.summary: Arsenal heeft zondag in de zestiende finale van het FA Cup-toernooi zeer overtuigend gewonnen van Wigan Athletic. The Gunners sloegen in het eerste halfuur vier keer toe, waarna krachten konden worden gespaard voor de titelstrijd in de Premier League.

items.title: Purpose and togetherness: What the soccer world can learn from FA Cup heroes Macclesfield
items.summary: Macclesfield produced one of soccer's greatest Cinderella stories in beating Crystal Palace in the FA Cup, but the tale behind the triumph is even more remarkable.

items.title: Turner orchestrates Man Utd comeback against London City Lionesses
items.summary: Millie Turner assists and then scores on her first start back from injury to help Manchester United come from behind and beat London City Lionesses 2-1.

items.title: Will Salt and Buttler fire for England against Italy?
Stevens geniet van Dick Schreuder: 'Fantastisch hoe hij NEC laat spelen'
items.summary: Huub Stevens stelt deze week in Voetbal International dat de Nederlandse clubs internationaal hebben gefaald en dat de lat flink omhoog moet. Wél geniet de oud-trainer van onder meer Schalke 04, HSV, Hertha BSC en VfB Stuttgart enorm van NEC en het voetbal dat Dick Schreuder zijn team laat spelen.

items.title: 'He's done it again!' | Chermiti completes stunning Rangers turnaround
items.summary: Youssef Chermiti puts Rangers ahead for the first time with a stunning header against Hearts

items.title: Sunderland latest: Le Bris hails energy and ambition in Oxford win
Bueno earns Wolves FA Cup win at muddy Grimsby
items.summary: Watch Premier League strugglers Wolves overcome a muddy pitch to beat Grimsby Town at Blundell Park and reach the FA Cup fifth round.

items.title: Bompastor admits pain of ‘emotional week’ after Chelsea beat Liverpool in WSL
items.summary: Chelsea continued to steady the ship as they secured a crucial victory against Liverpool in the Women’s Super League. Lauren James inspired Sonia Bompastor’s side to victory at Kingsmeadow, setting up Sjoeke Nüsken’s opener before making sure of the result with a goal in the second half. It has been a turbulent month for the Blues, both on and off the pitch. Back-to-back defeats against Arsenal and Manchester City all but ended their title defence and it was announced last week that the club had parted ways with Paul Green, their long-term head of women’s football. The news sent shockwaves through the game. Green had been part of the fabric of Chelsea for the past 13 years, playing an instrumental role in the success that included 19 trophies. Continue reading...

items.title: No notes: FA Cup GK is hero despite research fail
items.summary: Leeds United's Lucas Perri was the penalty shootout hero despite his preparation being foiled by Birmingham City.

items.title: FA Cup 'mud bath' survived by PL strugglers
items.summary: Wolves boss Rob Edwards described his side's 1-0 FA Cup win at Grimsby as an "aggressive game of headers and volleys" as the Premier League club survived a mud bath.

items.title: Macclesfield's Rooney: 'Ambition' to manage higher
items.summary: John Rooney is clear in his ambition to manage at the highest level possible but he wants to go on that journey with Macclesfield.

items.title: Barça's Flick praises ref 'good communication'
items.summary: Barcelona coach Hansi Flick said his chat with the referee from Thursday's 4-0 defeat to Atlético Madrid is a secret, but praised the communication.

items.title: India rout Pakistan at T20 World Cup to seal Super 8s spot
items.summary: India continued their T20 World Cup dominance of Pakistan with a thumping 61-run victory in Colombo sealing the defending champions and tournament favourites' spot in the Super 8s.
